title: Find the Maximum Element
statement: You are given a non-empty list of integers. Return the largest number present in the list.

title: Left Rotate Array by K
statement: Given a list of integers and a non-negative integer k, rotate the list to the left by k positions. Elements that fall off the left end wrap around to the right end. Return the rotated list.

title: Find All Duplicate Numbers
statement: You are given a list of n integers where every value is in the range [1, n]. Some numbers appear twice while others appear once. Return a sorted list of all numbers that appear more than once. If no duplicates exist, return an empty list.

title: Maximum Sum Contiguous Subarray
statement: Given a list of integers (which may include negative numbers), find the contiguous subarray with the largest possible sum and return that sum. The list will contain at least one element.

title: Minimum Jumps to Reach the End
statement: You are given a list of non-negative integers. Each element represents the maximum number of forward steps you can take from that position. Starting at index 0, return the minimum number of jumps required to reach the last index. It is guaranteed that reaching the last index is always possible.

title: Second Largest Unique Element
statement: Given a list of integers, return the second largest unique value in the list. If no such value exists (i.e., there is only one distinct value or the list has a single element), return -1.

title: Product of Array Excluding Self
statement: Given a list of integers, return a new list where the element at each index i equals the product of all elements in the original list except the one at index i. You must not use division in your solution.

title: Total Water Trapped Between Bars
statement: You are given a list of non-negative integers representing the heights of vertical bars, each of width 1, placed side by side. After rainfall, water collects between the bars. Calculate and return the total units of water that can be trapped.

title: Character Frequency Counter
statement: Given a string, return a dictionary where each key is a character from the string and its value is the number of times that character appears. Count every character including spaces and digits. If the string is empty, return an empty dictionary.

title: Group Words by Anagram Signature
statement: Given a list of words, group all words that are anagrams of each other into the same bucket. Return a list of groups where each group is a sorted list of words. Sort the final list of groups by the first word in each group.

title: Count the Lucky Sevens
statement: Write a recursive Python function count_sevens(n) that takes a non-negative integer n and returns the number of times the digit 7 appears in it. You are not allowed to convert the number to a string; you must use mathematical operations and recursion.

title: Recursive Peak Finder
statement: Write a recursive Python function find_max(arr) that returns the maximum integer in a given list arr. You must solve this strictly using recursion and without using Python's built-in max() function. Assume the list is never empty.

title: Alternating Recursive Sum
statement: Write a recursive Python function alt_sum(arr) that takes a list of integers and computes the alternating sum. The alternating sum subtracts the element at index 1 from index 0, adds the element at index 2, subtracts the element at index 3, and so on. If the array is empty, return 0.

title: Deep List Summation
statement: Write a recursive Python function deep_sum(nested_list) that calculates the sum of all integers in a deeply nested list. The list can contain integers or other lists, which in turn can contain integers or more lists, to any depth.

title: Non-Repeating Permutations
statement: Write a recursive Python function valid_permutations(s) that takes a string s and returns a list of all unique permutations where no two adjacent characters are exactly the same. The returned list must be sorted in lexicographical order.

title: Sort a List of Names Alphabetically
statement: Given a list of strings representing names, return them sorted in ascending alphabetical order. The comparison should be case-insensitive, but the original casing of each name should be preserved in the output.

title: Sort by Absolute Value
statement: Given a list of integers (which may include negative numbers), return the list sorted in ascending order of their absolute values. If two elements have the same absolute value, the negative one should come first.

title: Merge Two Sorted Arrays
statement: You are given two lists, each sorted in non-decreasing order. Merge them into a single sorted list in non-decreasing order and return it. Do not use any built-in sort function on the combined list.

title: Sort a List of Intervals by Start Time
statement: You are given a list of intervals, each represented as a pair [start, end]. Sort the intervals by their start times in ascending order. If two intervals share the same start time, sort by their end times in ascending order. Then merge all overlapping intervals and return the result.

title: Smallest K Numbers After Rearrangement
statement: You are given a list of n integers and a positive integer k (k <= n). You may perform at most one operation: pick any sublist of consecutive elements and reverse it. After optionally performing this operation, return the k smallest numbers present in the list in sorted order.

title: Find Position of a Target in Sorted List
statement: Given a sorted list of distinct integers in ascending order and a target value, return the index of the target using binary search. If the target is not present in the list, return -1.

title: Find First and Last Occurrence
statement: Given a sorted list of integers in non-decreasing order and a target value, return a list [first, last] representing the first and last index where the target appears. If the target is not found, return [-1, -1]. Your solution must run in O(log n) time.

title: Minimum Days to Complete a Task Quota
statement: A worker can complete at most speed s tasks per day. You are given a list of task bundles, where each bundle must be fully completed in one day. Given a quota q (total tasks needed), find the minimum speed s such that working through all bundles results in completing at least q tasks. Return that minimum speed.

title: Find the Rotation Pivot in a Shifted Sorted Array
statement: A sorted list of distinct integers was rotated at some unknown pivot index. Given this rotated list and a target integer, return the index of the target. If not present, return -1. Your solution must run in O(log n) time.

title: Allocate Minimum Pages
statement: You have n books arranged in a row, and each book has a certain number of pages. You must assign all books to exactly m students such that each student receives a contiguous segment of books. Minimize the maximum number of pages any student has to read, and return that minimum value.

title: Check if String is a Palindrome
statement: Given a string, determine if it reads the same forwards and backwards. Consider only alphanumeric characters and ignore case. Return True if it is a palindrome, False otherwise.

visible_tests.input: "A man a plan a canal Panama"
"Was it a car or a cat I saw"

title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array In-Place
statement: Given a sorted list of integers in non-decreasing order, remove duplicates in-place so each unique element appears exactly once. Return the number of unique elements.

title: Three Numbers Summing to Zero
statement: Given a list of integers, find all unique triplets (a, b, c) such that a + b + c = 0. The triplets must be returned in sorted order internally and the overall result must contain no duplicate triplets.

title: Container with Maximum Water Volume
statement: You are given a list of non-negative integers representing the heights of vertical lines drawn at each index. Two lines together with the x-axis form a container. Find the two lines that together hold the most water and return the maximum volume.

title: Shortest Subarray Making All Characters Balanced
statement: Given a string containing only the characters '(' and ')', find the length of the longest valid (balanced) parentheses substring.

title: Maximum Sum of K Consecutive Elements
statement: Given a list of integers and a positive integer k, return the maximum sum of any k consecutive elements in the list. If the list has fewer than k elements, return 0.

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
statement: Given a string, find the length of the longest substring in which no character appears more than once. Return 0 if the string is empty.

title: Minimum Window Covering All Required Characters
statement: You are given a source string s and a pattern string p. Find the smallest contiguous substring of s that contains all characters from p (including duplicates). Return the substring itself. If no such window exists, return an empty string.

title: Longest Subarray with Sum At Most K
statement: Given a list of non-negative integers and an integer k, find the length of the longest contiguous subarray whose sum does not exceed k. Return 0 if no such subarray exists.

title: Maximum Number of Non-Overlapping Subarrays with Target Sum
statement: Given a list of positive integers and a target sum t, find the maximum number of non-overlapping contiguous subarrays whose elements sum exactly to t. Greedily pick the earliest valid subarray each time.

title: Generate All Binary Strings of Length N
statement: Given a positive integer n, generate and return all binary strings of length n in lexicographic order.

title: All Subsets of a List
statement: Given a list of distinct integers, return all possible subsets (the power set) including the empty subset. The result must be in lexicographic order and must not contain duplicate subsets.

title: Letter Combinations from Phone Keypad
statement: A phone keypad maps digits 2-9 to letters. Given a string of digits, return all possible letter combinations in lexicographic order. Return an empty list if the input is empty.

title: All Valid Parentheses Arrangements
statement: Given a positive integer n, generate all strings containing exactly n pairs of parentheses that form valid bracket sequences. Return the results as a sorted list of strings.

title: Solve Sudoku Board
statement: You are given a 9x9 Sudoku board as a list of 9 lists, where empty cells are represented by '.'. Fill in the empty cells so that each row, each column, and each of the nine 3x3 sub-boxes contains the digits 1 through 9 exactly once. Modify the board in-place and return it.

title: Minimum Cost to Climb a Staircase
statement: You are given a list of integers where each element represents the cost of stepping on that stair. You can start from index 0 or index 1. From any stair, you may move up either 1 or 2 steps. Return the minimum total cost to reach just beyond the last stair.

title: Count Distinct Ways to Make Change
statement: You are given a list of distinct coin denominations and a target amount. Find the total number of distinct combinations of coins that sum exactly to the target amount. You may use each denomination an unlimited number of times. Return 0 if no combination is possible.

title: Maximum Points from Card Selection
statement: You are given a grid of integers with r rows and c columns. You start at any cell in the first row. At each step you may move to the cell directly below, or diagonally below-left, or diagonally below-right. You carry a penalty equal to the absolute difference in column index between consecutive steps. Return the maximum value of (sum of all collected cell values) minus (total movement penalty).

title: Shortest Path in an Unweighted Graph
statement: You are given n nodes numbered 0 to n-1 and a list of undirected edges. Given a source node src and a destination node dst, return the length of the shortest path (in number of edges) from src to dst. If no path exists, return -1.

title: Detect All Nodes in Cycles of a Directed Graph
statement: You are given n nodes numbered 0 to n-1 and a list of directed edges. Return a sorted list of all nodes that are part of at least one cycle. If no cycle exists, return an empty list.
